name: Apple Turnovers with Custard
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, combine the sugar and cornstarch. Stir in the milk until smooth.
2. Cook over medium-high heat until thickened and bubbly.
3. Reduce heat; cook for 2 minutes.
4. Remove from heat; stir into yolks.
5. Return all to the pan. Bring to a gentle boil; cook for 2 minutes.
6. Remove from heat; stir in vanilla. Cool slightly.
7. Cover with waxed paper and chill.
8. Place apples in a bowl; sprinkle with lemon juice. Add butter. Combine sugar, cinnamon, and cornstarch; mix with apples and set aside.
9. Divide pastry into 8 portions. Roll each into a 5-inch square.
10. Spoon filling off-center on each pastry.
11. Brush edges with milk. Fold over to form a triangle; seal.
12. Crimp with fork tines.
13. Make steam vents in the top.
14. Place on greased baking sheets.
15. Chill for 15 minutes. Brush with milk.
16. Bake at 400°F for 35 minutes.
17. Serve warm with custard.
